summary: Evaluate AI response drafting for support agent productivity
description:
Board has set a 20% cost-per-ticket reduction target for CX over FY26. Support team is the largest cost center within CX ($3.5M/yr loaded on the 50-agent team).

Objective: evaluate whether an AI drafting assistant can deliver meaningful (>$1M) annual productivity gains within a 12-month payback window, while keeping us inside our PII and tone-of-voice requirements.

In scope:
- Feasibility on our Salesforce + Snowflake stack
- 2-3 vendor proposals (ResponseAI received; two others TBD)
- Build-vs-buy analysis (org-level Data Science can advise but cannot lead)
- PII / privacy review

Out of scope:
- Voice / phone support automation (separate workstream)
- Tier-3 enterprise escalations (high judgment, low automation value)

Decision target: Q3 2026 funding committee.

comments.author: Daniel Ortiz
comments.body: Kicking this off — Priya, can you own discovery? Board wants the 20% cost-per-ticket target hit by end of FY26 so we need a clear path by Q3.

comments.author: Priya Sharma
comments.body: ResponseAI proposal received, attached. Their 70% acceptance claim feels high to me — most of their pilot was on retail shipping questions, very different from our enterprise tech mix. Need to pressure-test.

comments.author: Aisha Khan
comments.body: From Privacy perspective: any LLM that reads customer emails needs a clear data-residency story for EMEA tickets, and we need to confirm no customer PII is used for model fine-tuning outside our tenant. ResponseAI ToS is ambiguous on the second point — flag for review before contract.
